q: Fibre optics works on the principle of ___ internal reflection.
Fibre optics transmits information as ___ pulses along a glass or plastic fibre.
A fibre optic cable can contain from a few up to hundreds of ___ fibres.
An optical fibre is composed of two concentric layers, the ___ and cladding.
The ___ surrounds the glass fibre core in an optical fibre.
The cladding is surrounded by a layer called ___ (plastic buffer).
The core and cladding of an optical fibre have different ___ indices.
The refractive index of the core will be slightly ___ than that of cladding.
Light is total internally reflected if it strikes the core to cladding interface at an angle greater than the ___ angle.
The light ray continues ___ down the length of the fibre cable due to total internal reflection.
If the angle of incidence is less than the critical angle, both reflection and ___ take place.
Due to refraction and absorption, the light beam ___ and dies off over a certain distance.
A useful application of fibre optics is in ___.
Endoscopy is a ___ procedure used in medicine.
Endoscopy is used to examine the interior of a hollow organ or ___ of the human body.
___ use optical fibres to observe an image inside the body.
A bundle of optical fibres is inserted into the body to clearly monitor the ___ organs of the body.
